# teamcity_build_configuration_parameter (Resource)

Parameters are name=value pairs that can be referenced throughout TeamCity. Build configuration parameters are available to the specific build configuration. More info [here](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/teamcity/configuring-build-parameters.html)

Supports password-type parameters via the `type` attribute. When `type = "password"`, the parameter is stored securely in TeamCity and the `value` is treated as sensitive (not shown in plan/apply output). The provider uses TeamCity's secure parameter mechanism under the hood.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "teamcity_build_configuration_parameter" "bc_param" {
  build_configuration_id = "MyBuildConfigId"
  name = "env.MY_ENV_PARAM"
  value = "VALUE"
}
```

Password parameter example:

```terraform
resource "teamcity_build_configuration_parameter" "secret_param" {
  build_configuration_id = "MyBuildConfigId"
  name       = "secret_token"
  type       = "password"
  value      = var.secret_value  # kept sensitive by the provider
}
```

> Important note:
> 
> When using password parameter type and making changes from TeamCity UI, terraform provider will not be able to detect the change and will not update the parameter value because TeamCity server does not return sensitive passwords via REST API. Only changes via terraform provider will be properly shown in plan.

### Replacing an existing secure parameter on create

When defining a secret build configuration parameter resource (`type = "password"`) while one with the same name already exists in TeamCity, provider will show a warning that the previous value will be overwritten:

```
Warning: Existing secure parameter will be updated

A secure build configuration parameter named "<NAME>" already exists in build configuration "<BUILD_CONFIGURATION_ID>". Creating this resource will update (overwrite) the existing secure parameter value. TeamCity does not return secure parameters, the previous secure value will be lost.
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `build_configuration_id` (String)
- `name` (String)
- `value` (String, Sensitive)

### Optional

- `type` (String) Parameter type. Use `password` to create a secure (hidden) parameter. Defaults to `text` if omitted. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Computed

- `id` (String) Resource identifier in the form `build_configuration_id/name`.

## Import

```terraform
import {
  to = teamcity_build_configuration_parameter.bc_param
  id = "MyBuildConfigId/env.MY_ENV_PARAM"
}
```

> Important note:
>
> Password parameters cannot be imported because TeamCity server does not return sensitive values via REST API.
